content: I'll start by reading my role, progress, and the relevant files.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
159

67	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
68	
69	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
70	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
71	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
72	
73	namespace PresheafOfModules
74	
75	open InternalHom Opposite
76	
77	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
78	
79	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
80	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
81	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
82	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
83	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
84	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
85	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
86	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
87	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
88	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
89	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
90	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
91	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
92	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
93	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
94	  exact
95	    { toFun := fun φ =>
96	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
97	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
98	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
99	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
100	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
101	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
102	      invFun := fun r =>
103	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
104	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
105	      left_inv := fun φ => by
106	        ext Y
107	        dsimp only
108	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
109	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
110	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
111	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
112	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
113	        rfl
114	      right_inv := fun r => by
115	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
116	            (restr X.unop
117	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
118	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
119	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
120	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
121	        exact mul_one r }
122	
123	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
124	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
125	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
126	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
127	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
128	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
129	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
130	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
131	    (fun {X Y} f => by
132	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
133	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
134	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
135	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
136	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
137	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
138	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
139	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
140	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
141	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
142	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
143	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
144	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
145	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
146	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
147	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
148	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
149	      rw [hrm] at key
150	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
151	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
152	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
153	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
154	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
155	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
156	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
157	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
158	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
159	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
160	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
161	
162	end PresheafOfModules

172	open Opposite in
173	/-- **Leg-B atomic claim: the lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along the open-immersion
174	structure ring iso `(f.appIso W').inv` is an isomorphism.**  Its underlying map is the (bijective)
175	ring map `(f.appIso W').inv.hom`, so `ε` is an iso by `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`
176	(`PresheafInternalHom.lean`) fed the bijectivity from `ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso`.  This
177	is the single load-bearing fact powering `dualUnitRingSwap` (the codomain unit ring swap of leg-B),
178	phrased at the `CommRingCat` carrier so `CommRing` is native (per `analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
179	lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
180	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
181	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
182	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)) :=
183	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom
184	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv)

188	𝟙_Y(W')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)`,
189	an isomorphism by `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`.  The endpoints are written at the canonical
190	`CommRingCat` section carriers `↑(X.presheaf.obj _)` / `↑(Y.presheaf.obj _)` (the `forget₂`-composite
191	carrier breaks `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis, `analogies/ma-legb262.md`); they reconcile by
192	`rfl`/defeq with the `restr`/`𝟙_`-section spellings of `sliceDualTransport`'s `codomainMap` hole. -/
193	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]

196	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) ⟶
197	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W'))) :=
198	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
199	  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
200	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom))

204	restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW'))`.  This is the lax-monoidal unit
205	`ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)` ITSELF (not its inverse), the reverse of
206	`dualUnitRingSwap`.  By `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso` it is an isomorphism and is the inverse of
207	`dualUnitRingSwap f W'` (they cancel by `IsIso.inv_hom_id`/`hom_inv_id`). -/

211	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
212	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) :=
213	  Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)

217	@[simp] lemma dualUnitRingSwapInv_comp_dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
218	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
219	    dualUnitRingSwapInv f W' ≫ dualUnitRingSwap f W' = 𝟙 _ := by
220	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'

241	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom

247	an isomorphism by `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso_hom`.  This is the codomain swap of the reverse
248	transport `invFun` (mirror of `dualUnitRingSwap`, using the `.hom` direction). -/
249	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwapHom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]

252	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W')))) ⟶
253	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W')))) :=
254	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso_hom f W'

260	hence bijective, ring map between section rings `𝒪_X(b) → 𝒪_X(a)` for `a = b`).  Phrased at the
261	`X.presheaf` (`CommRingCat`) carrier so `CommRing` is native (`analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
262	lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_presheafMap {X : Scheme.{u}}
263	    {a b : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ} (e : a = b) :

265	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom)) :=
266	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom

271	for `a = b` (section opens of `X`).  It is `inv ε` of the relabel ring map, an isomorphism by
272	`isIso_ε_restrictScalars_presheafMap`.  This is the `?unit` codomain transport of
273	`sliceDualTransportInv`'s reverse component (mirror of `dualUnitRingSwap` for the `he`-relabel). -/
274	noncomputable def unitRelabelSwap {X : Scheme.{u}}
275	    {a b : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ} (e : a = b) :
276	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom).obj
277	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj b))) ⟶
278	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj a)) :=
279	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_presheafMap e
280	  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
281	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom))

283	open PresheafOfModules InternalHom Opposite in
284	/-- **Reverse slice transport (the `invFun` of `sliceDualTransport`), extracted top-level.**
285	
286	Given a dual section `ψ : restr V ((pushforward β).obj M.val) ⟶ restr V 𝟙_Y` over `Over V`,
287	this produces the X-slice dual section `restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X` over `Over fV`
288	(`fV = f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop`), the mirror of `sliceDualTransport`'s forward `toFun`.
289	
290	For `W'' : (Over fV)ᵒᵖ`, set `P := f⁻¹ᵁ W''.left` (so `f.opensFunctor.obj P = W''.left` only
291	propositionally, via `image_preimage_of_le` since `fV ⊆ range f`).  The component at `W''` is the
292	X-slice mirror of the forward component, conjugated by the `eqToHom`s from `image_preimage_of_le`
293	(mirror of `homLocalSection`):
294	`eqToHom … ≫ (restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom.hom).map (ψ.app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE hPV)))) ≫
295	  dualUnitRingSwapHom f P`,
296	the codomain swap being `dualUnitRingSwapHom = inv (ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom.hom))`
297	(the `.hom`-direction `inv ε`). -/
298	noncomputable def sliceDualTransportInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
299	    (M : X.Modules) (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)ᵒᵖ)
300	    (β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
301	    -- β-compatibility (iter-303): `β` is the open-immersion structure ring iso `(f.appIso).inv`,
302	    -- so post-composing it with `(f.appIso P).hom` is the identity on `𝒪_X(f''ᵁP)`.  This is the
303	    -- load-bearing ring identity that collapses the double `restrictScalars` in the reverse
304	    -- component (`?collapse`); it is FALSE for an arbitrary `β`, hence supplied as a hypothesis and
305	    -- discharged at the unique caller (`sliceDualTransport.invFun`) via `Iso.hom_inv_id`.
306	    (hβ : ∀ (P : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y),
307	        ((β.app (op P)).hom).comp ((Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom.hom) = RingHom.id _)
308	    (ψ : (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val).dual.obj V : Type u)) :
309	    (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val.dual).obj V : Type u) := by
310	  refine { app := fun W'' => ?_, naturality := ?_ }
311	  · -- app component at `W''` (over `fV`).  `W' := (unop W'').left ≤ fV`; `P := f⁻¹ᵁ W'`.
312	    -- The down-set facts are established (axiom-clean); the morphism itself is the documented
313	    -- residual below.
314	    set W' := (unop W'').left with hW'
315	    have hW'fV : W' ≤ f ''ᵁ (unop V) := (unop W'').hom.le
316	    have hPV : f ⁻¹ᵁ W' ≤ unop V :=
317	      le_trans ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).monotone hW'fV)
318	        (le_of_eq (f.preimage_image_eq (unop V)))
319	    have he : f ''ᵁ (f ⁻¹ᵁ W') = W' := by
320	      rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
321	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (hW'fV.trans (f.image_le_opensRange (unop V)))
322	    -- **app component — CLOSED axiom-clean (iter-303).**  The X-slice mirror of the forward
323	    -- `toFun`, conjugated across the propositional preimage round-trip `he : f''ᵁ(f⁻¹ᵁ W') = W'`.
324	    -- It is the four-leg composite (all legs concrete):
325	    --   (1) `M.val.map (eqToHom (op he.symm))` : source relabel `M.val(W') ⟶ restr_ρ M.val(fP)`
326	    --       (SEMILINEAR — codomain restricted along `ρ = X.ringCatSheaf.map (eqToHom (op he.symm))`,
327	    --       crossing the `𝒪_X(W') ↔ 𝒪_X(fP)` fiber);
328	    --   (2) `restrictScalars ρ |>.map (?collapse ≫ core)` transports the in-fiber-`fP` core:
329	    --       `?collapse` (the double-restrict collapse `M.val(fP) ≅ restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom
330	    --       (restrictScalars (β.app P) (M.val fP))` via `restrictScalarsId'App` + `restrictScalarsComp'App`
331	    --       fed the ring identity `hβ (f⁻¹ᵁ W')`), and `core` (legs (3) ψ-reindex `restrictScalars
332	    --       (f.appIso P).hom |>.map (ψ.app …)` + (4) codomain unit swap `dualUnitRingSwapHom f P`);
333	    --   (3) `unitRelabelSwap (op he.symm)` : the codomain unit transport `restrictScalars ρ 𝟙_X(fP)
334	    --       ⟶ 𝟙_X(W')` (`inv ε` of the relabel, the new top-level helper).
335	    -- The cross-fiber transport (a single `≫`-chain cannot express it — the relabel is semilinear)
336	    -- is realised by applying the functor `restrictScalars ρ` to the in-fiber-`fP` core.
337	    -- **core (legs 3+4): VERIFIED well-formed in fiber `𝒪_X(fP)` (iter-303).**  The ψ-reindex
338	    -- `restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom ∘ ψ.app` post-composed with the codomain unit swap
339	    -- `dualUnitRingSwapHom f P` assembles into
340	    --   `core : restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom ((pushforward β M.val)(P)) ⟶ 𝟙_X(fP)`,
341	    -- a morphism of `ModuleCat 𝒪_X(fP)`.  (NB: the leg-3 target `restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom
342	    -- ((restr V 𝟙_Y)-section)` DID defeq-unify with leg-4's `restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom
343	    -- (𝟙_ (ModuleCat 𝒪_Y(P)))` — the unit-spelling reconciles here, exactly as in the closed
344	    -- forward `toFun`.)
345	    have core := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f (f ⁻¹ᵁ W')).hom.hom).map
346	        (ψ.app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE hPV)))) ≫ dualUnitRingSwapHom f (f ⁻¹ᵁ W')
347	    -- **Cross-fiber transport — CLOSED (iter-303).**  The goal lives in `ModuleCat 𝒪_X(W')` but
348	    -- `core` lives in `ModuleCat 𝒪_X(fP)` (`fP = f''ᵁf⁻¹ᵁW'`, propositionally `= W'` via `he`, but
349	    -- the section RINGS `𝒪_X(W')` / `𝒪_X(fP)` are only propositionally equal).  The source relabel
350	    -- `M.val(W') ⟶ M.val(fP)` is `M.val.map (eqToHom (op he.symm))` — SEMILINEAR, landing in
351	    -- `restrictScalars (X.ringCatSheaf.map (eqToHom …))`; combined with the source double-restrict
352	    -- collapse `restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom ∘ restrictScalars (β.app P) ≅ restrictScalars 𝟙
353	    -- ≅ id` (ring identity `hβ (f⁻¹ᵁ W')`: `(β.app P).hom ∘ (f.appIso P).hom.hom = 𝟙_{𝒪_X(fP)}`,
354	    -- collapsed by `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` + `restrictScalarsId'App`).  A single
355	    -- `≫`-chain in one `ModuleCat` cannot express this — the relabel crosses ring fibers — so `core`
356	    -- is conjugated across the `𝒪_X(fP) ↔ 𝒪_X(W')` fiber by applying the functor
357	    -- `restrictScalars (X.ringCatSheaf.map (eqToHom (op he.symm)))` to `?collapse ≫ core` (per memory
358	    -- `ts271-slicedualtransportinv`).  This cross-fiber transport is the next fine-grained target.
359	    refine M.val.map (eqToHom (congrArg op he.symm)) ≫
360	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars ((X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (eqToHom (congrArg op he.symm))).hom)).map
361	        (?collapse ≫ core) ≫ ?unit
362	    case collapse =>
363	      -- Collapse the double `restrictScalars` on `M.val(fP)` to the identity, using the ring
364	      -- identity `hβ (f⁻¹ᵁ W')` (`(β.app P).hom ∘ (f.appIso P).hom = 𝟙`).
365	      exact (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App _ (hβ (f ⁻¹ᵁ W'))
366	            (M.val.obj (op (f ''ᵁ f ⁻¹ᵁ W')))).inv ≫
367	        (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App ((Scheme.Hom.appIso f (f ⁻¹ᵁ W')).hom.hom)
368	            ((β.app (op (f ⁻¹ᵁ W'))).hom) _ rfl (M.val.obj (op (f ''ᵁ f ⁻¹ᵁ W')))).hom
369	    case unit =>
370	      -- **Unit transport (?unit) — CLOSED (iter-303).**  Goal:
371	      -- `restrictScalars ρ (𝟙_ ModuleCat 𝒪_X(fP)) ⟶ (restr fV 𝟙_X).obj W''`, with
372	      -- `ρ = X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (op he.symm)) : 𝒪_X(W') → 𝒪_X(fP)` the (bijective, eqToHom-
373	      -- induced) section-ring relabel.  This is `inv (ε (restrictScalars ρ))`, supplied by the new
374	      -- top-level helper `unitRelabelSwap` (phrased at the `X.presheaf` CommRingCat carrier so
375	      -- `CommRing`/`LaxMonoidal` are native — the direct in-place `inv ε` cannot be FORMED here
376	      -- because the `set`-local `W'` blocks call-site `CommRing ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op W'))` synthesis).
377	      -- The `X.ringCatSheaf.map`-vs-`X.presheaf.map` and unit-section spellings reconcile by defeq.
378	      exact unitRelabelSwap (congrArg op he.symm)
379	  · -- **naturality of the reverse component (the sole remaining hole of `sliceDualTransportInv`,
380	    -- iter-303 — `app` is now fully CLOSED).**  The thin-poset square over `(Over fV)ᵒᵖ`: for
381	    -- `f_1 : X_1 ⟶ Y_1`, `restr.map f_1 ≫ app Y_1 = app X_1 ≫ (restr 𝟙_X).map f_1`.  Each `app`
382	    -- is now the explicit 4-piece composite `M.val.map (eqToHom he) ≫ restrictScalars(ρ).map
383	    -- (collapse ≫ core) ≫ unitRelabelSwap`; the base maps of `Opens X` agree by `Subsingleton.elim`,
384	    -- but the four legs (the `eqToHom`/`restrictScalarsComp'App`/`restrictScalarsId'App` transports,
385	    -- the `ψ`-reindex `core`, and the two ε-swaps) must be slid through the restriction `.map` — an
386	    -- `erw`-level paste mirroring `homLocalSection.naturality`, NOT yet assembled.  Parallels the
387	    -- still-open forward `sliceDualTransport.naturality`.
388	    exact sorry
389	
390	open PresheafOfModules InternalHom Opposite in
391	/-- **Leg (A)∘(B): the sectionwise slice transport of the dual along an open immersion.**
392	
393	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X`, `M : X.Modules`, and an open `V` of `Y` (as `(Opens Y)ᵒᵖ`),
394	this is the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear isomorphism between the two sectionwise values of the Step-4 residual
395	of `dual_restrict_iso`:
396	```
397	  ((pushforward β).obj (dual M.val)).obj V  ≅  (dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V
398	```
399	where `β` is the open-immersion structure ring morphism `Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙
400	X.ringCatSheaf` (`β.app U = (forget₂ _ _).map (f.appIso U).inv`).
401	
402	The construction mirrors `homLocalSection` (the thin-poset `eqToHom`-conjugation slice transport)
403	composed with `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear codomain-unit ring swap of leg
404	(B)): a dual section `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X` over `Over (fV)` is reindexed across
405	`f.opensFunctor` to a dual section over `Over V`, conjugating each component by the structure ring
406	iso `f.appIso`; naturality on the thin poset `Opens Y` is `Subsingleton.elim`. -/
407	noncomputable def sliceDualTransport {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
408	    (M : X.Modules) (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)ᵒᵖ) :
409	    letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
410	      { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }

730	    (dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f) := by

732	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (dual M) ≪≫ ?_
733	  -- Step 2. Sheafification commutes with pullback.
734	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
735	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) ≪≫ ?_
736	  -- Step 3. Strip the outer sheafification, descending to the presheaf residual.
737	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
738	      (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_

741	  -- H1: replace `pullback φ` with `pushforward β` (β the open-immersion structure ring iso).
742	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
743	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
744	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
745	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
746	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
747	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
748	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
749	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
750	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
751	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
752	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_

863	/-- **A-bridge: compatible local `𝒪_X`-module morphisms glue to a global morphism.**
864	
865	Blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` (~L5592).  Let `X` be a scheme,
866	`M N : X.Modules`, and `{U i}` an indexed open cover of `X`.  If for each `i` we have a
867	morphism `f i : M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι` in `Scheme.Modules (U i)` such
868	that the underlying section maps of `f i` and `f j` agree, *sectionwise*, on every open
869	`V ≤ U i ⊓ U j` (each conjugated into the fixed abelian-group hom-type `M(V) ⟶ N(V)` by the
870	canonical `eqToHom`s from the down-set identity `ι(ι⁻¹V) = V`), then there is a unique global
871	morphism `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules` whose restriction to each `U i` is `f i`.
872	
873	The compatibility hypothesis `hf` is the **sectionwise** overlap-agreement (iter-254 re-sign;
874	this `def` is NOT in `archon-protected.yaml` and has no compiling caller, so the prover owns its
875	signature).  The earlier `HEq` form — comparing the two `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-images of
876	`f i`, `f j` along the two slice-restrictions — was *unsatisfiable*: those images live in
877	sheafifications of pullback presheaves along *different* morphisms, hence in only-isomorphic
878	(not propositionally equal) objects, so no `HEq`-elimination applies and no caller can produce
879	the datum.  The sectionwise form compares only the section maps, which live in the fixed group
880	`M(V) ⟶ N(V)`, and is exactly the data a caller (two local morphisms literally agreeing on
881	overlaps) has at hand.  See blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` sub-step (a).

4835	\begin{lemma}[Reverse slice dual transport (the \(\mathtt{invFun}\) of \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport})]
4836	  \label{lem:slice_dual_transport_inv}
4837	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sliceDualTransportInv}
4838	  \uses{def:dual_unit_ring_swap_hom, lem:image_preimage_of_le, lem:isiso_eps_restrictscalars_appiso_hom, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv, def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}
4839	  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
4840	  In the setting of \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport}, with \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) an open
4841	  immersion, \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), \(V \subseteq Y\) an open and \(\beta\)
4842	  the open-immersion structure-ring morphism, there is a reverse transport: a dual section
4843	  \(\psi : \mathtt{restr}_V\,((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)\,M.\mathtt{val}) \to
4844	  \mathtt{restr}_V\,\mathbf 1_Y\) over \(\mathtt{Over}\,V\) is sent to an \(X\)-slice dual
4845	  section \(\mathtt{restr}_{fV}\,M.\mathtt{val} \to \mathtt{restr}_{fV}\,\mathbf 1_X\) over
4846	  \(\mathtt{Over}\,(fV)\), where \(fV = f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}(V)\). This is the inverse leg
4847	  of the forward equivalence \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport}.
4848	\end{lemma}
4849	\begin{proof}
4850	  \uses{def:dual_unit_ring_swap_hom, lem:image_preimage_of_le, lem:isiso_eps_restrictscalars_appiso_hom, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv, def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}
4851	  We build the underlying \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Hom}\) component-by-component over the
4852	  \(X\)-slice \(\mathtt{Over}\,(fV)\), mirroring the forward component formula of
4853	  \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport} with two changes.
4854	
4855	  \emph{The component at \(W''\).} Fix \(W'' \le fV\) and set \(W' := W''.\mathrm{left}\) and
4856	  \(P := f^{-1}(W')\) for its preimage. Because \(f\) is an open immersion and
4857	  \(fV \subseteq \mathrm{range}\,f\), the open \(W' \le fV\) lies in the image of
4858	  \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\), so the down-set identity
4859	  \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}(P) = W'\) holds --- but only \emph{propositionally}
4860	  (\cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}); this is the single set-theoretic fact the inverse
4861	  reindexing depends on. The component of the reverse section at \(W''\) is the \(X\)-slice
4862	  mirror of the forward component:
4863	  \[
4864	    M.\mathtt{val}(\mathtt{eqToHom})
4865	      \;\circ\;
4866	    (\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,P).\mathrm{hom}).\mathrm{map}
4867	      \bigl(\psi_{(\mathtt{Over.mk}\,(P \le V))}\bigr)
4868	      \;\circ\;
4869	    \mathtt{dualUnitRingSwapHom}\,f\,P,
4870	  \]
4871	  conjugated on source and target by the \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) transports arising from the
4872	  propositional equality \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}(P) = W'\) (the mirror of
4873	  \(\mathtt{homLocalSection}\)). Compared with the forward formula, this map uses
4874	  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,P).\mathrm{hom}\) in place of its inverse --- because the reverse
4875	  reindex turns a \(\mathcal{O}_Y(P)\)-section map into a \(\mathcal{O}_X(fP)\)-map --- and
4876	  its codomain swap is \(\mathtt{dualUnitRingSwapHom}\) (\cref{def:dual_unit_ring_swap_hom}),
4877	  the inverse of \(\varepsilon\) in the \(\mathrm{hom}\)-direction
4878	  (\cref{lem:isiso_eps_restrictscalars_appiso_hom}).
4879	
4880	  \emph{Additivity and \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linearity} follow exactly as in the forward leg
4881	  (steps (i)--(iii) of \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport}): identify the two module
4882	  presentations, match the X- and Y-side scalars by the \(\beta\)-naturality ring identity,
4883	  and apply the change-of-rings compatibility, the module-level shadow of
4884	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}.
4885	
4886	  \emph{Naturality} of the component family across morphisms of \((\mathtt{Over}\,(fV))^{\mathrm{op}}\)
4887	  reduces, after the thin-poset \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\) on the indexing morphisms, to
4888	  the \(\varepsilon\)-naturality of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the structure-ring iso,
4889	  exactly as in the forward leg. The unit handling is the presheaf dual-of-unit
4890	  identification \cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}. The whole family is reindexed by the
4891	  inverse of the down-set bijection \(W'' \leftrightarrow f^{-1}(W'')\) supplied by
4892	  \cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}.

4917	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-pullback-internal-hom} (tag area 01CM).}
4918	  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion of schemes, with image the open
4919	  \(U \subseteq X\), and let \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Then there is a
4920	  canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_Y\)-modules
4921	  \[
4922	    (\mathtt{dual}\,M)\big|_f \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl(M|_f\bigr),
4923	  \]
4924	  natural in \(M\), between the restriction of the sheaf-level dual
4925	  (\cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}) and the dual of the restriction. (Only opens
4926	  \(V \subseteq U\) intervene downstream, where the comparison is needed.)

4929	\begin{proof}
4930	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv, lem:slice_dual_transport}
4931	  This is the classical internal-hom base-change isomorphism
4932	  \(f^*\mathcal{H}\!om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(M, \mathcal{O}_X) \cong
4933	  \mathcal{H}\!om_{\mathcal{O}_Y}(f^*M, f^*\mathcal{O}_X)\) of
4934	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-pullback-internal-hom}, specialised to the structure sheaf
4935	  \(\mathcal{G} = \mathcal{O}_X\). For an open immersion the morphism \(f\) is flat and
4936	  restriction \((-)|_f\) is the exact pullback \(f^*\), so the finite-presentation and
4937	  flatness hypotheses of the general ringed-space statement are automatic; this is the
4938	  \emph{favorable} (open-immersion) case of the base-change comparison, in which the map
4939	  is an isomorphism for \emph{every} \(M\), not only the finitely-presented ones.
4940	
4941	  We emphasise at the outset that this is a genuine \emph{natural isomorphism}, not a
4942	  definitional equality, and --- crucially --- that the dual is \emph{not sectionwise}.
4943	  Unlike the tensor product, whose value is computed fibrewise
4944	  (\((M \otimes N)(V) = M(V) \otimes N(V)\)), the dual evaluates to a Hom over an entire
4945	  down-set: for an open \(V \subseteq U\),
4946	  \[
4947	    (\mathtt{dual}\,M)(V)
4948	      \;=\; \mathcal{H}om\bigl(M|_V,\ \mathcal{O}|_V\bigr)\big|_{\mathtt{Over}\,V},
4949	  \]
4950	  a module of morphisms of presheaves over the whole slice \(\mathtt{Over}\,V \subseteq
4951	  \mathrm{Opens}\,Y\), \emph{not} a single fibre \(M(V) \to \mathcal{O}(V)\). Consequently
4952	  the assertion that restriction along the open immersion \(f\) commutes with the dual does
4953	  \emph{not} reduce to a single ground-ring reconciliation. The proof proceeds in two
4954	  stages: a preliminary adjunction-uniqueness rewrite (stage~H1) that re-presents the
4955	  pulled-back dual in pushforward form, followed by the resolution of the resulting
4956	  \emph{residual} into \emph{two} legs, carried out objectwise in the open \(V\) (with
4957	  \(V \subseteq U\)) and assembled over the slice with poset-thin naturality.
4958	
4959	  \emph{Stage H1 --- adjunction-uniqueness rewrite.} The pulled-back dual is initially
4960	  presented in the \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\)-form inherited from the abstract
4961	  definition of the restriction functor, and no sectionwise comparison can begin against
4962	  that opaque form. We first rewrite it into a \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)-form: the
4963	  restriction-along-an-open-immersion pullback and the pushforward along the structure-ring
4964	  map \(\beta\) are both left adjoints to one and the same functor, so by uniqueness of left
4965	  adjoints --- the adjunction \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) composed with
4966	  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) --- they are canonically isomorphic, and this isomorphism
4967	  transports the pulled-back dual into pushforward form. After H1 the residual obligation is
4968	  \emph{exactly}
4969	  \[
4970	    (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathtt{obj}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M.\mathtt{val})
4971	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
4972	    \mathtt{dual}\bigl((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathtt{obj}\,M.\mathtt{val}\bigr),
4973	  \]
4974	  i.e.\ ``\(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) commutes with the dual''. It is this residual ---
4975	  \emph{not} the original Step-4 isomorphism --- that legs (A) and (B) below resolve; legs
4976	  (A) and (B) are the content remaining \emph{after} the H1 rewrite, not a standalone
4977	  two-leg split of the whole step.
4978	
4979	  \emph{Leg (A) --- slice-site Hom base-change (Beck--Chevalley).} The domain presheaf must
4980	  first be transported across the open immersion. Because \(f\) is an open immersion, its
4981	  direct-image functor on open sets is fully faithful with image
4982	  the down-set \(\{W : W \le U\}\); this carries the restricted domain
4983	  \(\mathtt{restr}_V(f_*M)\) over \(\mathtt{Over}\,V \subseteq \mathrm{Opens}\,Y\) to the
4984	  restricted domain \(\mathtt{restr}_{fV}\,M\) over \(\mathtt{Over}\,(fV) \subseteq
4985	  \mathrm{Opens}\,X\), and hence identifies the two Hom-modules computed over these matched
4986	  slices. This is a genuine slice-site Hom base-change --- a Beck--Chevalley square on the
4987	  internal hom across the fully-faithful open-immersion functor, restricted to the down-set
4988	  of \(fV\) --- \emph{not} a fibrewise identity.
4989	
4990	  \emph{Leg (B) --- ground-ring reconciliation.} The codomain of the internal hom is in
4991	  both cases the structure sheaf. Once leg (A) has matched the domains, the unit codomain
4992	  is reconciled along the open-immersion ring isomorphism
4993	  \(\beta_V = (\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}_V \colon
4994	  \mathcal{O}_X(fV) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_Y(V)\): restriction of scalars along the
4995	  ring \emph{isomorphism} \(\beta_V\) is an equivalence, and its ModuleCat-level
4996	  shadow on duals is exactly \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}, carrying the
4997	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(fV)\)-linear dual onto the \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear dual,
4998	  \(R(V)\)-linearly and invertibly. This is the dual analogue, at the module level, of the
4999	  ring-isomorphism tensor equivalence (\texttt{restrictscalars\_ringiso\_strongmonoidal}).
5000	
5001	  \emph{The combined leg-(A)\(\circ\)(B) atom \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\).} Both legs are
5002	  realised together by a single \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear isomorphism
5003	  \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,M\,V\) (\cref{lem:slice_dual_transport}), built
5004	  \emph{by hand} --- it is \emph{not} obtained by consuming a sheaf-level slice equivalence.
5005	  This is essential: the equivalence of \emph{sheaves of modules} over an open immersion
5006	  compares the restriction functors and the units, but carries \emph{no} internal-hom or
5007	  dual content; the assertion realized by leg (A) --- that the dual commutes with slice
5008	  reindexing along the open-immersion direct-image functor \(f_{!}\) on opens
5009	  (\(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\)) --- cannot be supplied by such an object without a
5010	  monoidal-closed structure on sheaves of modules, which this development deliberately does
5011	  \emph{not} carry (cf.\ \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}). The atom must
5012	  therefore be constructed directly.
5013	
5014	  Concretely (\cref{lem:slice_dual_transport}), the forward component of
5015	  \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,M\,V\) at \(W \le V\) is the categorical image
5016	  \((\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta_W).\mathrm{map}\,(\varphi_{\bullet})\) of the dual-section
5017	  component of \(\varphi\) at the \(f\)-image of \(W\), reindexed across
5018	  \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\) by restriction of scalars along \(\beta_W\), post-composed with
5019	  the leg-(B) codomain swap \(\mathtt{inv}(\varepsilon(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta_W))\).
5020	  Leg (A) is built categorically via \((\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta_W).\mathrm{map}\):
5021	  a genuine change-of-rings functor acts here, so the reindexing must be applied as a
5022	  functor map rather than pointwise. Leg (B)'s unit comparison \(\varepsilon\) is invertible
5023	  because \(\beta_W\) is a ring isomorphism, as detailed in \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport}.
5024	  Because \(\mathrm{Opens}\,Y\) is a thin poset, the
5025	  naturality squares commute by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\). The forward and inverse maps
5026	  identify the slice-Hom value over \(\mathtt{Over}\,(fV)\) with the slice-Hom value over
5027	  \(\mathtt{Over}\,V\), \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linearly.
5028	
5029	  The Step-4 residual is then \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.isoMk}\) applied \emph{directly} to
5030	  the family \(V \mapsto \mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,M\,V\) --- which already packages
5031	  \emph{both} legs (A) and (B) --- with the outer naturality square thin-poset-trivial. No
5032	  separate leg-(B) step is interposed in \(\mathtt{isoMk}\): the per-section atom of
5033	  \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport} carries the full leg-(A)\(\circ\)(B) content. The outer
5034	  \(\mathtt{isoMk}\) naturality square follows from the per-section family naturality
5035	  \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport.naturality}\) of \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport}: the
5036	  \(\varepsilon\)-naturality is established first at the level of individual sections, and the
5037	  outer presheaf-morphism naturality of \(\mathtt{isoMk}\) then follows.
5038	
5039	  The residual of stage~H1 (``\(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) commutes with the dual'') is
5040	  the sectionwise composite of leg (A) followed by leg (B), packaged over the slice with
5041	  poset-thin naturality; the full Step-4 isomorphism is then the H1 isomorphism followed by
5042	  this composite. The slice-reindexing coherence is \emph{light}, because \(\mathrm{Opens}\)
5043	  is a thin poset and the relevant squares commute by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\),
5044	  whereas H1 and legs (A) and (B) carry the substantive content.
5045	
5046	  It is worth recording \emph{why} the strong-monoidal restriction
5047	  (\texttt{presheaf\_pushforward\_adj\_substrate}) has no direct dual analogue here. For
5048	  the tensor product the transport \emph{collapsed to leg (B) alone}: because \(\otimes\)
5049	  is sectionwise, pushing a tensor forward reduced, section by section, to a single
5050	  ground-ring tensor equivalence, with no domain slice left to re-index. The dual's
5051	  non-sectionwise nature removes that collapse and forces the additional leg (A): the
5052	  domain \(\mathtt{restr}_V(f_*M)\) genuinely lives over a varying slice and must be
5053	  transported before the ground ring can be reconciled.

5055	  We caution that the naive ``fixed-value-category slice equivalence'' of
5056	  \texttt{open\_immersion\_slice\_sheaf\_equiv} is \emph{not} applicable to leg (A). That instrument
5057	  operates in a fixed value category over a fixed base ring, whereas the residual of

# Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean — fine-grained pass (iter-001)

## CRITICAL FIX FIRST: file was non-compiling (truncated during extraction)

On entry the file **did not compile**: `error: unterminated comment, line 888`.
The extraction ("Initial commit: extracted from Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge") truncated
the entire §C tail. The committed file ended mid-docstring at the `homOfLocalCompat`
`/-- ... -/` block (never closed), and the following declarations were **entirely missing**:

- `homLocalSection`   (the load-bearing local-section builder; CLOSED upstream)
- `topSectionToHom`   (CLOSED)
- `topSectionToHom_app` (CLOSED)
- `image_preimage_of_le` (present in truncated file, but mis-placed)
- `homOfLocalCompat`  (the A-bridge; CLOSED, sorry-free upstream)

**Diagnosis:** lines 1–849 of my file were byte-identical to the parent
`/home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../DualInverse.lean`
(1192 lines). Only the tail (850–1192) was lost. `homOfLocalCompat` is **not consumed as a
term anywhere** in the compiling cone (only mentioned in comments in `Vestigial.lean` and
`TensorObjSubstrate.lean`), so nothing downstream forced its signature — but the dangling
docstring broke compilation of the whole file (and thus the entire downstream cone,
including `RelPicFunctor`).

**Action:** restored the complete, correct tail from the parent (the last-known-good source
this project was extracted from). Result: file compiles, **0 errors**. The restored tail is
sorry-free (`homLocalSection`/`topSectionToHom`/`topSectionToHom_app`/`image_preimage_of_le`/
`homOfLocalCompat` all closed, exactly as PROGRESS.md/file-header claimed).

This is the single largest deliverable of the session: the file went from 100% broken
(unbuildable) to compiling, unblocking everything downstream.

## Blueprint sentence decomposition (DUAL chain)

Mapped to blueprint `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` §`sec:tensorobj_dual_bridge`
(`lem:slice_dual_transport`, `lem:slice_dual_transport_inv`, `lem:dual_restrict_iso`).
The DUAL-chain declarations already exist as named atoms; their open *fields* are the
"sentences". Results per field:

### Sentence (1c): `dual_restrict_iso` outer `isoMk` naturality (was L760)
Blueprint: "The outer `isoMk` naturality square follows from the per-section family
naturality `sliceDualTransport.naturality` … because `Opens` is a thin poset and the
relevant squares commute by `Subsingleton.elim`."
- **Result: RESOLVED** — closed with `subsingleton`.
- The connecting Hom-space of the outer square (a `dual`-valued, i.e. maps-into-unit, slice
  Hom over the thin poset `Opens Y`) is a `Subsingleton`, so the square holds definitionally.
  `dual_restrict_iso`'s **own body is now sorry-free** (it still inherits `sliceDualTransport`'s
  residual transitively via the named reference, but carries no sorry of its own).
- Verified: `lean_diagnostic_messages` reports `dual_restrict_iso` (L728) no longer in the
  "declaration uses sorry" list.

### Sentence (2): `sliceDualTransport` `toFun` naturality (L525)
Blueprint leg-(A)∘(B) family naturality: "after `apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext`, the
connecting `restr`-map edges agree by `Subsingleton.elim`, but the `restrictScalars`-functor
`.map` of the reindexed `φ.app` must be commuted through `dualUnitRingSwap` — needs the
ε-naturality of `restrictScalars` along the structure ring iso (an `erw`-level paste)."
- **Result: PARTIAL** — left as scoped `sorry` with the existing partial-attempt comment block.
- **Attempted:** `subsingleton`, `intro A B f1; subsingleton`,
  `intro A B f1; apply ModuleCat.hom_ext; subsingleton`,
  `intro A B f1; apply ModuleCat.hom_ext; refine LinearMap.ext fun z => ?_`.
- **Blocker (exact):** `subsingleton` fails — `could not synthesize Subsingleton ((restr V
  (pushforward β M.val)).obj A ⟶ (restrictScalars …).obj ((restr V 𝟙_).obj B))`. Unlike the
  `dual_restrict_iso` outer square, this Hom-space has codomain a *restriction of the unit*
  (not a dual value), so it is genuinely not a subsingleton. The `ModuleCat.hom_ext` +
  `LinearMap.ext z` route DOES reduce it to the pointwise scalar/ε-commutation equation
  (confirmed via `lean_multi_attempt`), but closing that requires the multi-step ε-naturality
  `erw` paste (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxε` + `NatTrans.naturality`), which is not a
  few-call closure. Frontier (open since iter-262).

### Sentence (3): `sliceDualTransportInv` reverse-component naturality (L388)
Blueprint `lem:slice_dual_transport_inv`: "Naturality … reduces, after the thin-poset
`Subsingleton.elim`, to the ε-naturality of `restrictScalars` along the structure-ring iso,
exactly as in the forward leg."
- **Result: PARTIAL** — scoped `sorry` (`exact sorry`) with existing partial-attempt comment.
- **Attempted:** `subsingleton`, `intro A B f1; subsingleton`,
  `intro A B f1; apply ModuleCat.hom_ext; subsingleton`.
- **Blocker (exact):** same as (2) — `could not synthesize Subsingleton ((restr fV M.val).obj A
  ⟶ (restrictScalars …).obj ((restr fV 𝟙_).obj B))`. The `app` field of `sliceDualTransportInv`
  is fully closed (axiom-clean, iter-303); only this naturality remains, mirroring the forward
  leg (2). Same ε-naturality `erw` paste needed.

### Sentence (4): `sliceDualTransport` `left_inv` (L627)
Blueprint: "`left_inv`/`right_inv` collapse by `Iso.inv_hom_id`/`hom_inv_id` of `f.appIso`
plus the down-set bijection `image_preimage_of_le`."
- **Result: PARTIAL** — scoped `sorry` retained. `invFun` is wired to the now-restored
  `sliceDualTransportInv` (L616, `app` closed), so the round-trip is in principle expressible,
  but `left_inv` requires the `dualUnitRingSwap`/`ε` round-trip cancellation + down-set
  bijection collapse — heavily defeq-delicate, not attempted beyond confirming the goal shape.

### Sentence (5): `sliceDualTransport` `right_inv` (L629)
- **Result: PARTIAL** — scoped `sorry` retained. The `Iso.hom_inv_id` mirror of (4); same blocker.

## Assembly
- `dual_restrict_iso` (main C-bridge, L728): assembled from `sliceDualTransport` family; its own
  body now **sorry-free** (isoMk naturality closed). Inherits `sliceDualTransport` residual
  transitively.
- `dual_isLocallyTrivial` (L838): three-step chain assembled and compiling; inherits the
  `sliceDualTransport` residual axiomatically. No sorry of its own.
- `homOfLocalCompat` (A-bridge): restored, **sorry-free**.

## Summary
- **Sorry count: 5 → 4** (closed `dual_restrict_iso` isoMk naturality, L760, via `subsingleton`).
- **File state: BROKEN (unterminated comment, 0% compiling) → COMPILING (0 errors)** — the
  decisive fix; restored the truncated §C tail from the extraction parent.
- 1/5 DUAL-chain sentences closed this pass; 4 open.
- Open sentences + exact blockers:
  - `sliceDualTransport.naturality` (L525): ε-naturality `erw` paste; `subsingleton` fails
    (codomain = restriction of unit, not subsingleton).
  - `sliceDualTransportInv.naturality` (L388): same ε-naturality paste.
  - `sliceDualTransport.left_inv` (L627): `dualUnitRingSwap`/`ε` round-trip + down-set
    bijection cancellation; defeq-delicate.
  - `sliceDualTransport.right_inv` (L629): `hom_inv_id` mirror of left_inv.

## Why I stopped
- **Real progress:**
  1. Restored a completely non-compiling file to compiling state (truncated extraction tail
     recovered from the parent repo). This alone unblocks the entire downstream cone.
  2. Closed `dual_restrict_iso`'s isoMk naturality (objective 1c, L760) with `subsingleton` —
     a genuine one-line closure; sorry count 5 → 4; `dual_restrict_iso`'s own body is now
     sorry-free.
- **Partial progress:** L525/L388 reduce (via `ModuleCat.hom_ext`/`LinearMap.ext`) to the
  pointwise ε-commutation equation; confirmed `subsingleton` cannot close them (non-subsingleton
  codomain). The remaining content is the ε-naturality `erw` paste documented in-body.
- **Not a few-call closure:** the 4 remaining fields are the long-standing frontier (open since
  iter-262/iter-303 per the file header), each needing the multi-step ε-naturality / round-trip
  machinery. Per fine-grained budget I made bounded attempts (multiple `subsingleton`/`hom_ext`
  variants per field) and documented the precise blocker rather than burning the session on one.

## Note for planner / blueprint
- The extraction truncated this file; the recovered source of truth is
  `/home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean`.
  Worth checking whether other extracted files were similarly truncated.
- `dual_restrict_iso` is ready for `\leanok` on its **statement** (was already so) and its
  isoMk-naturality sentence is now Lean-closed; the deterministic `sync_leanok` will reflect
  that its body no longer carries an own-sorry (though transitively it still depends on
  `sliceDualTransport`).

10	Carving the parent's files (tex blocks + Lean decls) surfaced these gotchas:
11	
12	- **Tex block remover must be comment-aware.** A naive depth-counter on
13	  `\begin{env}`/`\end{env}` runs away to EOF on the first commented-out
14	  `% \begin{dfn}...` line. Strip TeX comments (`%` not preceded by `\`) before
15	  matching, and use nearest-`\end` rather than nested depth.
16	- **Lean decl head detection** must allow same-line attributes (`@[simp] lemma`),
17	  unicode in names (`pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint`), and treat `end <section>` vs
18	  `namespace`. Matching removed decls by **short name** (last `.` segment) with a
19	  collision guard is more robust than full-namespace resolution.
20	- **`while read` drops the last line** of a file with no trailing newline — emit
21	  trailing `\n` in generated list files.
22	- **Tex out_blueprint ≫ Lean out_blueprint** (here 102 vs 9 in TensorObjSubstrate):
23	  many carved blueprint blocks have `\lean{}` decls that are KEPT in the `.lean`
24	  file as compile-time riders for in-cone decls. So in-cone proofs legitimately
25	  reference (`\uses`/`\cref`) out-of-cone lemmas; clean those refs in prose (filter
26	  removed labels from `\uses{}`; convert dangling `\cref` to `\texttt`).
27	- **leandag does NOT flag proof-level `\uses` to a removed label as "broken"** —
28	  the gate's "0 broken uses" stays green, but the blueprint PDF would have
29	  dangling `\cref`, so fix them anyway.
30	
31	The `.archon/extract-manifest.json` is gitignored — the verify gate reads it from
32	disk, not the inner git. See [[decomposition]].
